tools.name: GPTZero
tools.confidence_note: Real-world FPR 15%: Ryne.ai, 100k+ texts (2025), via eyesift.com — commercial source, treat as indicative. NNES 61%: Liang et al., Cell Patterns 2023, peer-reviewed, high confidence.
tools.key_finding: Under adversarial editing, accuracy across 7 detectors drops to 17.4% (Perkins et al., 2024). PeerJ 2025: flags 25% of NNES AI-assisted texts as 100% AI vs ~10% for native speakers.

tools.name: Turnitin
tools.confidence_note: 4% sentence-level FPR: Turnitin's own admission. NNES +30%: multiple peer-reviewed studies. ACU: 25% of 6,000 cases dismissed (ABC News, Oct 2025). Real-world accuracy: 85–90% vs 98% claimed.
tools.key_finding: Australian Catholic University: ~6,000 AI misconduct allegations in 2024, ~25% dismissed, Turnitin AI detection abandoned March 2025. Self-admits 20% threshold rule due to internal unreliability findings.

tools.name: ZeroGPT
tools.confidence_note: FPR 28%: Pangram Labs benchmark. Detection accuracy: Perkins et al. Sept 2024 (46.1%), Weber-Wulff Dec 2023 (59%). Both peer-reviewed or rigorous independent studies.
tools.key_finding: PeerJ CS 2025 (Pratama): ZeroGPT and DetectGPT 'misclassified nearly half of texts — performing no better than random guessing' on controlled dataset.

tools.name: Originality.ai Turbo
tools.confidence_note: FPR 37%: RAID benchmark (Dugan et al., ACL 2024), largest and most rigorous published benchmark. Accuracy 80%: same source. Vendor claims based on proprietary benchmarks.
tools.key_finding: Largest gap between vendor claim (0.2% FPR) and independent measurement (37% FPR) of any tool in RAID benchmark — a factor of 185x.

benchmark_sources.name: Liang et al. (2023)
benchmark_sources.finding: Average 61.3% false positive rate on TOEFL essays; one detector flagged 98% of non-native English writing as AI-generated

benchmark_sources.name: Dugan et al. (2024) — RAID Benchmark
benchmark_sources.finding: 6M+ texts, 11 models, 11 adversarial attacks: detectors struggle at safe false positive rates and collapse under simple adversarial editing

benchmark_sources.name: Pratama (2025)
benchmark_sources.finding: ZeroGPT and DetectGPT perform near random; top tools flag 25% of NNES AI-assisted texts as 100% AI vs ~10% for native speakers

benchmark_sources.name: Stowe et al. (2025)
benchmark_sources.finding: Across 16 detectors: non-white ELL essays disproportionately classified as machine-generated; economically disadvantaged students' essays classified as machine-generated less often

benchmark_sources.name: Jung et al. (2025) — FairOPT
benchmark_sources.finding: Fixed detection threshold causes systematic group disparities; shorter texts and 'neurotic' writing styles disproportionately flagged

harm_cases.outcome: Abandoned Turnitin AI detection March 2025. Deputy VC acknowledged figures 'substantially overstated'.

French-born EMBA student Thierry Rignol: 1-year suspension, failing grade. Showed same tool flagged Yale president's 30-year-old writings as AI. Federal lawsuit pending (D. Conn. 3:25-cv-00159). Injunction denied May 2025.

PhD student Haishan Yang expelled; student visa revoked; career and residency ended. Expulsion upheld by Minnesota Court of Appeals (Feb 2026). Federal lawsuit dismissed (Oct 2025). First documented US PhD expulsion for AI use.
